name: dorado_basecaller
description: Basecall nanopore data. Use this for standard basecalling and modified base detection.
usage: dorado [-h] [--device VAR] [--read-ids VAR] [--resume-from VAR] [--max-reads VAR] [--min-qscore VAR] [--batchsize VAR] [--chunksize VAR] [--overlap VAR] [--recursive] [--modified-bases VAR...] [--modified-bases-models VAR] [--modified-bases-threshold VAR] [--emit-fastq] [--emit-sam] [--emit-moves] [--reference VAR] [--kit-name VAR] [--barcode-both-ends] [--no-trim] [--trim VAR] [--sample-sheet VAR] [--barcode-arrangement VAR] [--barcode-sequences VAR] [--primer-sequences VAR] [--estimate-poly-a] [--poly-a-config VAR] [-k VAR] [-w VAR] [-I VAR] [--secondary VAR] [-N VAR] [-Y] [--bandwidth VAR] [--junc-bed VAR] [--mm2-preset VAR] model data

pos_args.name: model
pos_args.description: Basecalling model. ALWAYS prefer 'sup'. Example: rna004_130bps_sup@v5.2.0.

pos_args.name: data
pos_args.description: Input POD5 or FAST5 path. MUST be a real file path (e.g., ./data, /path/to/fast5). Do NOT use placeholders like 'data'.

kwargs.modified-bases: String. Example: m6A_DRACH (NOT list) (e.g., m6A_DRACH). Used with --modified-bases.
kwargs.modified-bases-models: Path(s) to custom models. Used with --modified-bases-models. Note: The --modified-bases-models in Dorado typically follows the naming convention: [Base Model Name]_[Modified Name]@v[Version Number]. If the user requests m6A_DRACH, please refer to the model naming format provided in the context or RAG results. If the precise model path cannot be determined, please leave it blank in ToolCall and allow the Validator node to automatically complete it.
kwargs.recursive: Set to true if data is a directory.
kwargs.emit-moves: Write the move table to the 'mv' tag. Ture or False.

name: dorado_aligner
description: (Optional) Alignment uses `minimap2` and by default uses the `lr:hq` preset. This can be overridden by passing a minimap option string, `--mm2-opts`, using the '-x ' option and/or individual options such as -k and -w to set kmer and window size respectively. Usage: dorado aligner [--help] [--recursive] [--output-dir VAR] [--emit-summary] [--bed-file VAR] [--threads VAR] [--max-reads VAR] [--verbose]... [--mm2-opts VAR] index reads
usage: dorado aligner [-h] [--recursive] [--output-dir VAR] [--emit-summary] [--bed-file VAR] [--threads VAR] [--max-reads VAR] [-k VAR] [-w VAR] [-I VAR] [--secondary VAR] [-N VAR] [-Y] [--bandwidth VAR] [--junc-bed VAR] [--mm2-preset VAR] index reads

pos_args.name: reads
pos_args.description: An input file or the folder containing input file(s) (any HTS format). [nargs=0..1] [default: ""].

name: dorado_summary
description: (Optional) Only used when the user explicitly requests to view basecalling statistics or generate a summary file.

name: dorado_download
description: (Optional) Download or view all current models in Dorado, and you can also download specific models.

args.list: list the available models for download
